methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Apis Cor is a seed-stage construction technology company that has raised $5.5M to date. The Housing & Construction sector contains a limited peer universe of two private companies (ICON, BotBuilt) at various stages; no public comps exist in this narrow category.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum sample thresholds (≥3 datapoints). Using total capital raised ($5.5M, proxied as $6M) as a base, a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $14M. This approach is grounded in observed progression patterns across the seed funding cohort, though it does not incorporate sector-specific comparable transaction multiples.

The supported valuation range is $12M to $16M, with a median point of $14M. This estimate carries material uncertainty stemming from: (1) the single-method reliance due to sparse peer data, (2) the absence of Housing & Construction sector precedents at seed stage, and (3) dependence on the $6M proxy conversion from raised capital. Refinement of this range would benefit from either identification of additional early-stage construction-tech peers or transaction evidence from ICON or BotBuilt exits. Until then, the $12M–$16M band should be treated as indicative rather than definitive.
